lede: A look at November 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Shah Rukh Khan and James K. Polk, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Vienna
events.desc: In Vienna's Innere Stadt district, an ISIL sympathizer shoots and kills four people and injures 23 more, before being shot and killed by the police.

events.title: Lewis Hamilton
events.desc: Lewis Hamilton secured his maiden Formula One Drivers' Championship Title by one point ahead of Felipe Massa at the Brazilian Grand Prix, after a pass for fifth place against the Toyota of Timo Glock on the final lap of the race.

events.title: Expedition 1
events.desc: Expedition 1 arrived at the International Space Station for the first long-duration stay onboard. From this day to present, a continuous human presence in space on the station remains uninterrupted.

events.title: Tropical Storm Linda (1997)
events.desc: Tropical Storm Linda makes landfall in the Mekong Delta in Vietnam, causing more than 3,000 deaths.

events.title: Capital punishment
events.desc: Capital punishment: Velma Barfield becomes the first woman executed in the United States since 1962.

events.title: Dutch–Indonesian Round Table Conference
events.desc: The Dutch–Indonesian Round Table Conference ends with the Netherlands agreeing to transfer sovereignty of the Dutch East Indies to the United States of Indonesia.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: First day of Battle of Elaia–Kalamas between the Greeks and the Italians.

events.title: Balfour Declaration
events.desc: The Balfour Declaration proclaims British support for the "establishment in Palestine of a national home for the Jewish people" with the clear understanding "that nothing shall be done which may prejudice the civil and religious rights of existing non-Jewish communities".

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Russian Empire declares war on the Ottoman Empire and the Dardanelles is subsequently closed.

events.title: North Dakota
events.desc: North Dakota and South Dakota are admitted as the 39th and 40th U.S. states.

births.name: Shah Rukh Khan
births.line: Indian film actor, producer and television host

births.name: James K. Polk
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 11th President of the United States

births.name: Rafiq Tarar
births.line: Pakistani judge and politician, 9th President of Pakistan

births.name: Warren G. Harding
births.line: American journalist and politician, 29th President of the United States

births.name: Marie Antoinette
births.line: Austrian-French queen consort of Louis XVI of France

births.name: Esha Deol
births.line: is an Indian actress who appears in Hindi films.

deaths.text: Acker Bilk, English singer and clarinet player
Hal Roach, American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Pier Paolo Pasolini, Italian actor, director, and screenwriter
Ngo Dinh Diem, President of South Vietnam from 1955 to 1963
George Bernard Shaw, Irish author, playwright, and critic, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: All Souls' Day (Roman Catholic Church and Anglican Communion)
